text.title: structured-output-mode-picker Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that result from picking the wrong constrained-decoding mode.

antipattern.symptom: Pydantic parse fails on roughly every malformed batch (~5-15% of rows); failure is intermittent and lacks a deterministic signature.
antipattern.root-cause: Pipeline uses `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` for extraction that downstream code parses into typed models. json-mode guarantees only valid JSON, not schema compliance.
antipattern.fix: Switch to SO strict (`response_format={"type": "json_schema", "strict": true, "schema": ...}`). See [[strict-mode-required-fields]] for the schema invariants.

antipattern.symptom: SO strict schema is a discriminated union of action shapes; user code dispatches on the discriminator field; parallel tool calls are impossible.
antipattern.root-cause: Author modeled agent actions as data instead of using tool-call mode's native function-name dispatch.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to tool-call: each action becomes a tool with its own `input_schema`; the provider returns the chosen function name in the response. Supports parallel calls; no custom router needed.

antipattern.symptom: SQL / regex / custom-DSL is generated inside a tool-call argument as a single unconstrained string. Output is syntactically broken ≥10% of the time.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrapped a DSL workload in tool-call mode because grammar mode was unfamiliar.
antipattern.fix: Use grammar mode (XGrammar / Outlines / GBNF) on a model that supports it. XGrammar reports 100x throughput vs string-inside-JSON workarounds.

antipattern.symptom: Outlines / XGrammar configured with a JSON-Schema grammar for typed extraction; throughput is fine but Pydantic parse still fails on edge cases (`additionalProperties` not enforced).
antipattern.root-cause: Grammar mode was chosen for a JSON consumer; strict SO would have been cleaner.
antipattern.fix: Stay on grammar mode only if the provider lacks strict SO; otherwise switch to provider-native SO strict (smaller surface, deterministic). Migrate when strict SO becomes available.

antipattern.symptom: Service uses SO strict on OpenAI and falls back to tool-call on Anthropic for the same logical task. Output shapes drift between providers.
antipattern.root-cause: Author let each provider drive the mode pick instead of fixing the consumer-driven choice up front.
antipattern.fix: Pick the mode by consumer first, then map to each provider's nearest equivalent. Document the mapping in the decision-record; track providers that lag for migration.
